meta.description: Meta Brain — NeuroGrim scoring itself. Dogfooding the LSP Brains spec.
meta.note: Run sensory tools with: neurogrim sensory <tool> --project-root . > .claude/<tool>-cmdb.json

config._todo_subagent-health: Reserved for multi-agent task completion scoring. Add back once a Rust sensory tool exists; the name is preserved in VISION.md + docs as a design target.

domain_definitions._doc: test-health, code-quality, and deploy-readiness are weighted. git-health and rust-health are advisory (weight=0.0) — visible in health output but don't affect the score until promoted.
domain_definitions._todo_rust-health: Advisory domain for Rust-specific signals: clippy lint count, cargo audit CVEs, unused deps, MSRV compliance. Activate: add scoring_source pointing to a custom sensory tool output, set weight > 0. See sdk-python/ for writing a custom sensory tool.

agent-behavior._doc: Non-deterministic verification of agent behavior per LSP-Brains spec §15. Scenarios live at .claude/agent-behavior-scenarios/ (currently a stub; ecosystem-level library is the authoritative set). Refresh: `neurogrim cast agent-behavior --project-root . > .claude/agent-behavior-cmdb.json`. Advisory-only in v1.

skill-coherence._doc: B-11 (2026-04-22, drift-detection scope): byte-level coherence between duplicated skill files across sibling/child Brains in the fractal topology. Refresh: `neurogrim sensory skill-coherence --project-root . > .claude/skill-coherence-cmdb.json`. Advisory-only in v1.

capability-hygiene._doc: B-12 (2026-04-22, contracted scope): scores skill description-block quality against the authoring standard in write-skill.md. The 1,536-char description block IS the routing contract in Claude Code's native skill index. Refresh: `neurogrim sensory capability-hygiene --project-root . > .claude/capability-hygiene-cmdb.json`. Advisory-only in v1.

supply-chain-sca._doc: E-SC-2 (2026-04-24): native-Rust Software Composition Analysis for crates.io ecosystem. NO external scanner binaries (no Trivy, no cargo-audit binary, etc.). Pipeline: cargo-lock parse → OSV.dev batch + 24h file cache → RustSec-advisory-db pinned-submodule local cross-reference → operator-accepted-advisories filter → count-based scoring. Refresh: `neurogrim sensory supply-chain-sca --project-root neurogrim > .claude/supply-chain-sca-cmdb.json` (note: project_root points at the cargo workspace subdir; the sensor falls back one level up to find .claude/ and vendor/). Override 24h OSV cache with NEUROGRIM_OSV_NO_CACHE=1. Operator acceptances live in .claude/supply-chain-accepted-advisories.toml. Advisory weight 0.0 for v1; publish-gate uses own-dep score==100 as a binary check, not a numeric contribution. Master gate per BEFORE-PUBLIC-RELEASE.md §11.

supply-chain-vigilance._doc: E-SC-5 (2026-04-25): native-Rust Layer 2 deep-signal vigilance per LSP-Brains v2.6 §16.3. Seven sub-sensors: typosquat-proximity (Levenshtein ≤1 to top-popular), publish-cadence (acceleration + post-dormancy), maintainer-delta (new maintainer in 30d window), transitive-surface-delta (dep-count surge), signature-gap (attestation drop), binary-reproducibility (registry-checksum verification — opt-in via NEUROGRIM_VIGILANCE_REPRO=1), exfil-indicator (base64/eval/exec/subprocess static analysis on extracted tarballs — opt-in via NEUROGRIM_VIGILANCE_EXFIL=1). Direct registry JSON-API queries (crates.io / PyPI / npm-registry) with 7-day file cache; override via NEUROGRIM_VIGILANCE_NO_CACHE=1. NO external scanner binaries. Refresh: `neurogrim sensory supply-chain-vigilance --project-root neurogrim > .claude/supply-chain-vigilance-cmdb.json`. Per-package historical state for maintainer-delta lives at .claude/brain/cache/vigilance/state/. Advisory weight 0.0 in v1 — promotion past advisory requires §15.5-equivalent calibration evidence (E-SC-8). Findings are advisory; never auto-gate per §16.3.

supply-chain-review._doc: E-SC-6 (2026-04-26): Layer 3 review framework per LSP-Brains v2.6 §16.4. supply-chain-auditor hat + decision-ledger writer + review-ticket file format + supply-chain-review CMDB sensor + AUTO-CREATE bridge from Layer 2 vigilance findings (dedup by ecosystem/package/finding_kind). Score model v1: 100 - 10 × open_tickets, capped 0. Default weight 0.0 (advisory) — promotion past advisory requires §15.5-equivalent calibration evidence (E-SC-8). Read-only static analysis MUST per §16.4: framework never downloads or extracts package source. Human decision is the gate: `neurogrim sca-review resolve` requires non-empty --note + valid --decision (accept|reject|pin-to-last-good|no-action). Append-only discipline: triage entries supersede pending entries via supersedes_ts. v1 has NO automated LLM invocation — agent_findings are operator-edited (agent piece is post-v1 follow-on). Refresh: `neurogrim sensory supply-chain-review --project-root neurogrim > .claude/supply-chain-review-cmdb.json`. Decision ledger: .claude/supply-chain-decision-ledger.jsonl. Tickets: .claude/brain/supply-chain-tickets/.

domain-calibration._doc: E-B2-2 (2026-04-27): Brains-2.0 per-domain calibration meta-observer per LSP-Brains v2.8 §17.9. Reads all .claude/brain/*-calibration-ledger*.jsonl files and reports per-domain calibration health. Score model v1: 100 - 10 × open_pending_total, floor 0. Default weight 0.0 (advisory) — promotion past advisory requires §15.5-equivalent calibration evidence. Tuple-aware (has_ever_fired, last_triage_age) confidence per §17.9: distinguishes 'no signal yet' (high confidence) from 'recently triaged' (high confidence) from 'pending unanswered' (low confidence). Bootstrap-loop guard: this sensor's OWN calibration trigger MUST be hard-coded Manual (recursion guard enforced in build_scorecard, E-B2-2 C7). Refresh: `neurogrim sensory domain-calibration --project-root neurogrim > .claude/domain-calibration-cmdb.json`. Calibration ledgers: .claude/brain/<domain>-calibration-ledger.jsonl.

trust-budget._doc: E-B2-4 (2026-04-27): Brains-2.0 trust budget primitive per LSP-Brains v2.10 §16.8. Reads trust-budget.toml at repo root (Q5 lock — committed, NOT under .claude/) and validates against trust-budget-v1.schema.json. Cross-references declared_crates / declared_shell_outs / declared_external_services against the actual surface (cargo lockfile direct deps per Q1 lock; bash function-aware shell-out scan per C3-fix; FQDN scan of *.rs/*.sh/*.py source). Emits two-sided drift findings per Q8: trust_budget:undeclared:* and trust_budget:overdeclared:*. Closed-set vocabulary per Q1: ecosystem ∈ {cargo, pypi, npm, system}; trust_posture ∈ {api_only, official_registry, operator_audited, vendor_attested}. Composes with §5.4.1 hat-contracts: reads forbidden_tools[] + network_targets.allowed[] from each persona-hat. Default weight 0.0 (advisory) at v1; hard gates deferred to v2 per BACKLOG B-23 (calibration-gated promotion mirrors §16.4 advisory-before-strict). Refresh: `neurogrim sensory trust-budget --project-root <brain> > .claude/trust-budget-cmdb.json`.

operator-calibration._doc: E-B2-6 (2026-04-27): Brains-2.0 operator-calibration primitive per LSP-Brains v2.11 §17.12. Sibling family of domain-calibration (§17). Reads .claude/brain/invocation-ledger.jsonl and recognizes BOTH SkillEntry rows (PostToolUse hook writer per Axis 4 v1) AND DispositionEntry rows (`neurogrim disposition record` CLI writer). Closed-set 4-entry disposition vocabulary per Q1: accepted, rejected, modified, superseded. Score = (accepted_count / dispositioned_count) × 100 when dispositioned_count >= N_MIN=20; null below threshold (Q9 sample-size disclosure MUST). Aggregation-only export per Q5 + E6-1 BR-5 mitigation — no per-invocation rows in CMDB. Recursion guard per Q6: operator_calibration:* finding kinds rejected by disposition CLI as invocation_id targets. Privacy contract reaffirmed: NO free-text justification (additionalProperties:false on DispositionEntry enforces structurally). Default weight 0.0 (advisory) at v1; v2 hard gates deferred per BACKLOG B-23 with §15.5 evidence-bundle review. Refresh: `neurogrim sensory operator-calibration --project-root <brain> > .claude/operator-calibration-cmdb.json`.

correlations.name: deploy-without-test-baseline
correlations.description: Deploy pipeline is healthy while test baseline is degraded — can ship, cannot verify.
correlations.insight: Raise test-health score above 40 before treating deploy-readiness as a reliable signal.

correlations.name: commit-debt-with-quality-pressure
correlations.description: Uncommitted changes accumulating while code quality signals are degraded — both suggest development pressure without discipline checkpoints.
correlations.insight: Commit in smaller increments and run lint/format before staging. Both signals share the same root cause.

correlations.name: security-gate-missing-from-ci
correlations.description: Deploy capability exists but security baseline is unestablished — pipeline can ship without any security validation.
correlations.insight: Add a SECURITY.md and at least one SAST workflow step before promoting deploy-readiness weight.

executive.description: C-suite and stakeholders — score + top risk only

manager.description: Engineering managers — domain breakdown + gate status

specialist.description: Domain experts — filtered view emphasizing relevant domains

engineer.description: Active development — emphasize test health and build quality

reviewer.description: Code review — emphasize quality and completeness
